description: A16FJ - Day 5, Part 2: Lunar Module Undocking and Descent Preparation; Revs 11 and 12

tokens: This is Apollo Control; 94 hours, 21 minutes into the flight of Apollo 16.
Less then a minute away now from acquisition of data and voice from the spacecraft.
Coming around now part way through the eleventh lunar revolution.
The communications with the two spacecraft have been split at this time, that is a separate CapCom or spacecraft communicator will be talking to Ken Mattingly aboard Casper.
Ed Mitchell is the Command Module CapCom.
Jim Irwin remains as CapCom to communicate with Young and Duke aboard Orion.
During this eleventh revolution, the - We have Acquisition Of Signal.
Let's stand by for communications with Orion.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: Although we have two different CapComs, the transcripts do not differentiate between them until after 094:44.
After that, the Technical Transcript separates out the communications between MCC and the CM and MCC and the LM.
However, before then, it is not possible to determine with absolute confidence which CapCom is speaking, except when the Apollo 16 crew address the CapComs by name.
The transcript will simply identify the speaker as "CapCom" until 094:44.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Ole Orion; how do you read?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We're zipping right on through the checklist.
We got the PGNS up, the docked coarse align done, the landing gear's deployed, and the only thing we haven't done is really what you need to see.
And we're ready to start in on the S-Band checks and bring up the steerable.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: The high gain dish antenna on the LM is known as the steerable antenna, which, like the HGA on the CSM, can be manually or automatically steered to point at Earth.
Its articulation system will prove to be faulty, a failure that will have repercussions for the rest of the LM's life.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Okay, while guidance is - Let me give you some angles.
We had a Verb 06 Noun 20 that was done at 94:20:20.
The LM angles were plus 29465, plus 28996, plus 35502.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: At 094:20:20, the LM crew asked the computer to display (V06) the current angles from the IMU (N20).
The returned angles were +294.65°, +289.96° and +355.02° in roll, pitch and yaw respectively.
These would be stated relative to the orientation of the platform at that time, based on it being coarsely aligned the Landing Site REFSMMAT.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Copied the LM as plus 29465, plus 28996, plus 35502.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: The Command Module are plus 00269, plus 10931, plus 00472.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: It sounds like we've got good comm on Primary S-band and T/R and Secondary Power Amp.
I'm going Secondary S-Band T/R and Primary Power Amp.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I think it's my switch you're doing.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We want narrow on the S-band.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: How do you read the old CDR?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, Jim; how do you read?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I read you, but there's a lot of noise in the background.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: We have Primary Evap activation time as 93:33:30; and we're standing by for the AGS abort constants.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: ...let's do the steerable first and get - and get you the downlink off the E-memory dump.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We're standing by for the steerable.

tokens: I have a PIPA bias update for you.

transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: The pitch should be 99 on the steerable.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: We got the aft Omni, I can't - when I move the yaw drive, the yaw indicator does not move on the steerable, and I do not hear any grinding noise.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Roger, I copy no response on the yaw drive.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: if you're ready, Charlie, I'll read you the AGS abort constants.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, 224 beginning: plus 60511, plus 29419, plus 60383, plus 00565, minus 32776, minus 54412.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I can't seem to get the yaw out of the stowed position, minus 12 on the indicator, and I cannot hear it drive when I move this dial - just like the - in pitch I can hear it drive and the needle follows.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, I - We read you, Charlie.
We want you to go through a little procedure here to essentially get you back to a start position.
We want you to go to Slew on the S-Band.
Check both S-Band circuit breakers on 11 and 16.
Select Pitch of minus 75 and Yaw of minus 12: wait 30 seconds and then go to an acquisition and we'll have some angles for you.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And, Orion, the angles for your S-band are Pitch of 99 and Yaw of 16.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I have that PIPA bias for you whenever you're ready to copy.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Irwin's conversation with Ken is missing from the audio recording presented here, presumably due to the splitting of the mission control functions between LM and CSM, and the recorder only recording the LM conversations.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Okay, on the PIPA bias: Address 1454, Data 03521; Address 1456, Data 76274.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, on the 1456, couldn't hear your readback, Ken.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And, Casper, this is Houston with a DAP load for you whenever you're ready.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, I'm in Block, and I'm standing by.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Will you select Down Voice Backup...
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: We're reading you but still excessive noise down here.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Was it as noisy on the other transmitter receiver and power amp?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I think it was about the same, Charlie.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, be advised - we went through the steerable Step 2 on Page 3-62, and the yaw still does not indicate that it's moving, and we cannot hear a grind.
Though, with those angles, I get a - that I got of 99 and minus 12, I get a signal strength of greater than 3.

I went Track Mode to Auto and UpLink - and when I went Track Mode to Auto, it sounded like the thing is setting up there just constantly oscillating and the - the pitch needle varies plus or minus 10.
The signal strength stays pretty constant.
In fact, no matter what position I select on the S-Band antenna, the signal strength stays up around 38 or so.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Could we press on with the Aft Omni in - in this configuration?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Can you get an E-memory dump?
We're ready for the E-memory dump.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, let's bypass that because we need high bit rate.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: You can't get high bit rate on the omni?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Not until we get to the - 210 up.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Irwin is referring to a 210-foot (64-metre) dish antenna.
The two omnidirectional antenna on the LM do not have sufficient gain to allow high bit rate data to Earth if being received by the usual 85-foot (26-metre).
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: [Pause.] Okay, we'll bypass the UpLink and we'll go on and do the - the Ascent Battery checkout.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, we're ready for the battery checkout.

tokens: [Pause.] Okay, we're preparing a REFSMMAT for you now, John, that you'll have to put in manually.
Then you'll be able to proceed.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: A heavy sigh is heard on the air/ground audio, probably from John who recognises that having to work through a low-gain antenna is going to make the upcoming task a lot more onerous.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: I have a - a P27 for you, one for REFSMMAT and the other's your state vector.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Orion, we - we'd like you to go back to Slew and place the antenna at the Stow position, minus 75 and minus 12 and just leave it there.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And, Charlie; if you're ready to copy, I have this P27 update for the REFSMMAT and state vector for you.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: At 094:44 a separate communications loop is established between Casper and Mission Control.
To make the transcript more readable, the communications relevant to Casper and Orion will be presented as two separate blocks between 094:44 and 095:27.
Each will indicate the other only where necessary to cover communication between the two spacecraft.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: We start with communications between Orion and Mission Control; Casper's communications restarthere.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Okay, that's a good readback, I have your state vector for you when you're ready.

tokens: The yaw is still not working.

I guess we still don't have high bit rate, huh?

tokens: On this RCS problem - we want you to transfer out of System A down to a reading of 80 percent on RCS System A.
We are trying to transfer out of that system to reserve your blowdown capability.
We would like you to go ahead and transfer now, but do not let the aft pressure go above 180.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, closed, 80 percent, and the aft pressure's 19 - 155.

You have a Go to do that Hot Fire Check on the back side.
We'd like you to use System A.
Of course, the procedure for that is to close Main SOV [Shut Off Valve] on System B and to open the Crossfeed so that you'll be using just System A.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: For your hot fire check on the back side, we want you to use system A.
So we'd like you to close the Main SOV System B and open the Crossfeed.

We no longer need Min Dead Band.

Okay, read those numbers to me.

transcript.speaker: Irwin

And our Noun 06 is at 95:23 and 24.
For Command Module, plus 268, plus 10919; plus 005, 00503.
And the torque was about a minute before that on the - on the P52.

"PGNS Rate Command (Cold Fire), AGS pulse." CB 11, Attitude Direct, Close.

transcript.speaker: Duke

I got the CSM DAP load, which is copied at 95:55.

Okay, go ahead with your PADs.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: And, Hank, Jim's still up on our loop.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: And we ought to orbit down here at eight miles.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Roger; I had your P24 PAD for about 96:45.
I think we've got time to get that in.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: I thought 16-3 was to be used only for high altitude.
That's kind of a large crater, isn't it?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Stand by, and I'll check it.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Maybe that's the only one that's in the track.
Okay, let me read it back to you, and just verify that later.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: And I'm going to go out the window here for just a minute.
And I've got the VHF off so I can talk to you.
If the LMs call me or something, you might give me a call.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Okay, I'm coming up on the edge of the Kant Plateau now and, from down here, that looks like a real steep rise.
But there's nothing that's particularly significant about the topography or the texture, it just rises up.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: And all the surface here, Hank, has this striation effect to it.
It's like we were looking at down there at - pictures of the Silver Spur and so forth.
But this is on the horizontal surface.
And it all has that same effect.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Okay, Hank, we slipped by it.

Coming up upside down and backwards almost vertical like this, I didn't get a very good hack at it, but I did get a - one look at it, and you can identify North and South Ray and all the features there, and got a look at that old Erastostenion [sic] Crater that's over to the southwest that shows beautiful lineaments in the photographs.
And they aren't there visually from - from here.
It looks like it has the same interior that all the rest of the things do.
And I'll talk more about that later.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Okay, I'm ready to go to work.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Did you get any photos in there?
transcript.speaker: CapCom

tokens: Just had one glance at it before it was occulted by the LM.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Ken, did you get a Flight Plan change at a hundred hours and 20 minutes to charge Battery A?
transcript.speaker: CapCom

tokens: Okay, I couldn't tell from here whether that'd gone up or not.
That - At a hundred hours, 20 minutes, we'd like to add "Charge Battery A."
transcript.speaker: CapCom

tokens: Okay, charge Battery A at a hundred hours and 20 minutes, and I'm gonna go get my helmet and gloves on here.
Then we'll do a little suit circuit integrity check.
Got anything that we ought to do first?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Did you happen to take a look at that docking latch?
Okay, I've just been told that's all squared away.
So I don't - don't know of anything that needs to be done now before you go ahead with the integrity check.
transcript.speaker: CapCom

tokens: I'm going to press into that.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: For your information, the LM's about four or five minutes from hot fire.
transcript.speaker: CapCom

tokens: Okay, I'm still on a tight dead band.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: I was just giving you a warning, in case you wanted to bring your VHF up or whatever you need to do to talk to them.
transcript.speaker: CapCom

tokens: I understand you're coming up on the hot fire check, and I think I'm supposed to go to a Wide Dead Band.
Can I have about two minutes to complete my suit check?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: If you've got a sec there, I'd like to give you your sep PAD.
Stand by; I'll wait until you get through with the check.
transcript.speaker: CapCom

tokens: I'll wait until you get through with your integrity check.
Give me a call when you're through, please.
transcript.speaker: CapCom

tokens: There's not much else I can do.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Okay, I'm still in Min Dead Band.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Okay, Casper's completed with a suit circuit integrity check.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

transcript.speaker: CapCom

tokens: And your attitude is good except pitch is 105.
transcript.speaker: CapCom

tokens: And in regard to that question you had about 16-3, your present groundtrack is about 15,000 feet south of the preplanned, and that puts 16-1 outside the acceptable limits; 16-3 is closer to the landing site, and they would like for you to go ahead and use that.
transcript.speaker: CapCom

tokens: And I guess the message is there, Ken, just shoot the center.
transcript.speaker: CapCom

tokens: There appears to be parts missing of a conversation between Ken Mattingly and Orion's crew over the following few minutes.
This is probably due to the poor comms, and the lack of either LM or CM on-board recordings for this period.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Okay, I don't have it Off yet, John.
What happened to our hot fire?

transcript.speaker: Duke

transcript.speaker: CapCom

tokens: We're about two minutes from LOS.
transcript.speaker: CapCom

tokens: Orion, I am in Wide Dead Band, Att Hold.
Okay, I'll go Free when you give me the call.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: We've got about a minute until LOS.
transcript.speaker: CapCom

tokens: We've had Loss of Signal toward the end of the eleventh lunar revolution of Apollo 16.
Still docked at this time, however, by the time the two spacecraft come around the front side of the Moon again at the start of the 12th revolution they will have undocked.
That is scheduled for Ground Elapsed Time of 96:13, about four minutes, correction six minutes prior to first acquisition on orbit number 12.
As the spacecraft went around the corner, Duke and Young were conducting the Reaction Control System hot-fire checks aboard Lunar Module Orion.
There has been some difficulty in getting the steerable S-Band antenna on Orion to perform - particularly to yaw to the proper settings for providing good communications to Earth.

However, for Revolution 13, which is the landing revolution, we will have the 202 foot - 210 foot [64-metre] large communications dish at Goldstone, California in acquisition of the spacecraft and all the high bit rate data, voice and all of the forms of communication with Orion can be handled through the omni-antennas, provided we do have the 210-foot antenna.
There was some imbalance between Reaction Control System regulator pressures between Systems A and B.
That was the reason why the crew was instructed to open the crossfeed to allow some of the propellant from System A to go into the Ascent [Stage] propellant storage tanks.
The propellant is not lost in this operation.
It merely is transferred to another tank.
Also the crew is instructed to use System A only for the RCS hot fire, also to take some of the load off of the RCS System A.
Some 45 minutes away from acquisition in revolution Number 12.
At LOS, Apollo 16 at [sic] an orbit measuring 10.3 by 58.9 nautical miles [19.1 by 109.1 kilometres].
And at 95:32 Ground Elapsed Time, this is Apollo Control.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: We return to a combined transcript with Loss of Signal at 095:28.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: This is Apollo Control at 95 hours, 45 minutes Ground Elapsed Time.
Some 31 minutes away from acquisition of Apollo 16 as it starts around the front side of the Moon on orbit number 12.

To summarize the apparent problem with the Lunar Module steerable antenna, if by the time of powered descent and landing the problem has not sorted itself out and the antenna is, indeed, out of commission (right now it appears that the crew cannot get the antenna to rotate in the yaw movement), it will be possible for all data and voice, high bit rate data, to be relayed to Earth through the omniantennas starting with revolution number 13 which is the landing revolution.
The 210-foot [64-metre] antenna in Goldstone, California will be the prime site.
There's no apparent concern here in Mission Control with the potential loss of the steerable antenna.

Some mention as been made of the fact that the meters that indicate antenna position sometimes fail or hang in the one indication, when actually, the antenna is properly rotating, but over the next two revolutions, this situation should sort itself out.
At any rate, loss of the antenna does not mean that the landing will be aborted.
We're still in a Go situation.
And at next acquisition, the two spacecraft, Casper and Orion, will have been separated by some six minutes prior to acquisition.
28 minutes now until Acquisition of Signal from Casper and Orion.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: Quoted as "You guys got any problems" on CM tape.
transcript.speaker: Commentary
